exercises.prompt: What makes an éditorial different from a news report?

options.text: It takes a stance with the paper's full authority
It only lists verified facts
It is always anonymous and neutral

exercises.explanation: Un texte argumentatif signed by the house: the editorial IS the paper's opinion.

exercises.prompt: In a DALF synthèse, your personal opinion…

options.text: is worth zero points — objectivity is the rule
is the most important part
goes in the first paragraph

exercises.explanation: The synthèse stays silent; the essai next door is where you argue.

exercises.template: Le sommet fait la {{b1}} de tous les quotidiens.
exercises.explanation: Faire la une = to make the front page.

exercises.prompt: First step of the synthèse method.
exercises.template: {{b1}} d'abord l'idée générale de chaque document.
exercises.explanation: Dégager l'idée générale — one line per document. Recopier is the cardinal sin.

exercises.prompt: Two summaries stapled together instead of a dialogue between sources. The grader says…

options.text: the documents were never confronted — that's not a synthèse
perfect, that's the expected format
too short, add a third summary

exercises.explanation: Confronter: stage where the documents agree and where they collide.

exercises.template: La règle d'or de la synthèse : l'{{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: L'objectivité — no stance, no « je », no verdict.

exercises.prompt: Match each synthèse step to its job.

pairs.right: what type of document it is
to draw out the main idea
to say it in your own words

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order: "The editorial was published on the occasion of the summit."
Listen to the examiner's warning. What mistake is she describing?
exercises.transcript: Vous avez écrit une excellente synthèse — malheureusement, le sujet demandait un essai : ici, votre opinion était la bienvenue.

options.text: Delivering one genre to the other's door: a synthèse where an essai was asked
Writing too many pages
Quoting the documents word for word

exercises.explanation: The two genres face each other: synthèse = no opinion, essai = your opinion. Read the sujet.

exercises.prompt: Read this account of the 2009 editorial and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Le 7 décembre 2009, cinquante-six quotidiens de quarante-cinq pays ont publié à la une le même éditorial, paru à l'occasion du sommet de Copenhague : « douze jours pour changer notre monde ». Un « nous » planétaire prenait position pour l'action immédiate. Geste inédit dans l'histoire de la presse — et cas d'école pour l'analyse : l'occasion est datée, la position est explicite, et la question de savoir qui parle n'a jamais été aussi vertigineuse.
exercises.passageEn: On 7 December 2009, fifty-six dailies from forty-five countries published the same editorial on their front pages, on the occasion of the Copenhagen summit: 'twelve days to change our world'. A planetary 'we' took a stance for immediate action. An unprecedented gesture in press history — and a textbook case for analysis: the occasion is dated, the position is explicit, and the question of who is speaking has never been so dizzying.

options.text: Fifty-six dailies published the same text on their front pages
It contained no opinion
It was never actually published

questions.prompt: Why is « qui parle ? » called vertiginous here?

options.text: The editorial « nous » is usually one paper — here it claimed to be the planet
The authors were anonymous hackers
Nobody read the text

exercises.prompt: A good problématique for a synthèse pairing a pro-action editorial with a sceptic scientist's column:

options.text: « Un consensus peut-il être mis en doute ? »
« Pourquoi le scientifique a-t-il tort ? »
« Quel document est le plus long ? »

exercises.explanation: The question both documents argue over — without deciding it for them.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Cinquante-six quotidiens, une seule une : l'éditorial de Copenhague.

options.text: Fifty-six dailies, one front page: the Copenhagen editorial.
An editorial takes a stance; a synthesis stays silent.
Draw out the nature, the main idea, then the shared problématique.
an editorial
the front page
a daily

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « L'affaire fait la une des quotidiens. » — the front page.
